disposition_rationale: Principal clarified that passive income should not be collapsed into manual sales or into diversification across agent-skill vendors. The correct abstraction is a portfolio of self-serve income mechanisms: agent/developer tooling, market-modeling assets, data/API products, and research/content licensing.
disturbs: Atlas expand/park/retire framing when disconnected from income-sleeve strategy
Any future synthesis that treats marketplace proliferation as portfolio diversification
evidence: Principal statement 2026-05-21: 'We should be diversifiying our passive income at a higher abstraction level than just different skill vendors.'
Existing Launchpad Lint MONETIZATION_CHANNELS.md already warns not to treat one marketplace as the business.
Atlas CURRENT_STATE.md shows a stopped market-behavior modeling loop that should be evaluated as a separate passive-income sleeve rather than idle project noise.
next_step: Have skillfoundry and atlas project sessions update their local strategy/current-state docs around passive-income portfolio sleeves and propose first measurable passive-income experiments.
summary: Treat passive income as a portfolio-level objective across different self-serve income mechanisms, not merely as distribution across agent-skill marketplaces. Agent tooling, atlas market-modeling assets, data/API products, and research/content licensing should be evaluated as separate sleeves with distinct evidence and risk profiles.
title: Passive income portfolio, not skill-vendor diversification
